prose:
**Fukui reactivity analysis of nitrobenzene** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**, density-fitted by chemkit default) in PySCF, **gas phase** (no implicit solvent), neutral reference at **charge 0, multiplicity 1**. Geometry: user-supplied `.xyz` (`/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz`, 14 atoms), used as-is — a single point at the given geometry; it was NOT re-optimized as part of this task. Partial-charge scheme: Mulliken (PySCF). The three needed N±1 states were computed as: cation (charge +1, mult 2, UKS) and anion (charge −1, mult 2, UKS).

**Method used:** condensed-to-atom Fukui functions from finite-difference of Mulliken atomic charges between the neutral, cationic, and anionic species, all at B3LYP/def2-TZVP in the gas phase.

**Headline result — condensed Fukui indices:**
- **Most electrophilic site** (largest f⁺, susceptible to nucleophilic attack): atom index 4 (1-based), a **C**, with **f⁺ = 0.49641779**. This is the headline value reported.
- **Most nucleophilic site** (largest f⁻, susceptible to electrophilic attack): atom index 5 (1-based), a **C**, with f⁻ = 0.1072882.

The dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) is likewise largest in magnitude and positive at C4 (0.51070983), consistent with it being the most electrophilic site.

**Integrity / convergence:** the engine reports `integrity.status = "ok"` and **`trustworthy = true`**. All three integrity checks passed. The warnings (all severity "warning") are reproduced verbatim:
- fukui_charge_conservation_f+ : "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)" — passed (ok=true).
- fukui_charge_conservation_f- : "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)" — passed (ok=true).
- schema_header : "common header present" — passed (ok=true).

**Caveats per reporting standards:** (1) Fukui values are model-dependent and Mulliken-based, so absolute magnitudes are basis-set sensitive; treat them as relative-reactivity trends, not measured quantities. (2) Density fitting (RI) is an approximation to the two-electron integrals, on by chemkit default. (3) Gas phase only — no solvent, no explicit solvation. (4) Result is at a single, fixed input geometry (no conformer averaging, no DFT re-optimization). 

**Reproducibility:** command was `.../cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 <nitrobenzene.xyz> --out .../live.json`. A Fukui plot was written to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/argo_claude-opus-4.8/20260703-194529_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`. No unsolicited experimental comparison is offered.
